content.problemStatement:
The score of an array BBB of length MMM is defined as follows:


Let PPP denote the number of pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that 1≤i<j≤M1 \leq i \lt j \leq M1≤i<j≤M and Bi=BjB_i = B_jBi​=Bj​.
Then, score(B)=M−P\text{score}(B) = M - Pscore(B)=M−P.
You are given an array AAA of length NNN.

Your task is to compute the **maximum** score across all contiguous subarrays of AAA, i.e. compute the quantity

max⁡1≤L≤R≤Nscore([AL,…,AR])\max_{1 \leq L \leq R \leq N} \text{score}([A_L, \ldots, A_R])1≤L≤R≤Nmax​score([AL​,…,AR​])
Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the length of the array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum score across all contiguous subarrays of AAA.
constraints: The sum of NNN across all tests won't exceed 5⋅1055\cdot 10^55⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : When L=1,R=4L = 1, R = 4L=1,R=4, then we get M=4M = 4M=4 (length), and P=0P = 0P=0 as there are no equal pairs. This gives us a score of 444 and it can be proven that we can't do better.
Test Case 2 : score([2,4,1])=3−0score([2, 4, 1]) = 3 - 0score([2,4,1])=3−0, and this is a subarray of [2,4,1,4][2, 4, 1, 4][2,4,1,4] by considering L=1,R=3L = 1, R = 3L=1,R=3.
